s: 곰곰이 means 'thinking something over carefully' in Korean
e: '곰곰이' means to think something over carefully and thoroughly, taking time to reflect before concluding.

s: 국산 means 'domestically produced' in Korean
e: '국산' means domestic production — goods made within the country, as opposed to imported products.

s: 한참 means 'for a good while / quite a long time' in Korean
e: '한참' means a considerably long stretch of time — 'for a good while' or 'for quite some time.'

s: 나름 means 'depending on / it's up to' in Korean
e: '나름' means depending on — it expresses that the outcome varies based on context or person, as in '사람 나름이다' (it depends on the person).

s: 갈수록 means 'more and more / increasingly' in Korean
e: '갈수록' means as time goes on, things become more and more so — intensification over time.

s: 반신반의 means 'half believing, half doubting' in Korean
e: '반신반의' literally means half trust, half doubt — describing a state of uncertainty, neither fully believing nor fully disbelieving.

s: 끊기다 means 'to be cut off / to end abruptly' in Korean
e: '끊기다' is the passive form of 끊다 (to cut/end), meaning to be cut off or terminated — used for calls, streams, or connections.

s: 별일 means 'nothing special / no particular issue' in Korean
e: '별일' means nothing special or no particular matter. Most commonly heard as '별일 없어?' (Nothing special going on?), a casual greeting.

s: 주한 means 'stationed in Korea / based in Korea' in Korean
e: '주한' is a prefix meaning stationed in Korea, used in contexts like 주한미군 (US Forces Korea) or 주한 외교관 (ambassador stationed in Korea).

s: 꼼짝없이 means 'helplessly / with no way out' in Korean
e: '꼼짝없이' means being trapped or stuck with no way to escape — helplessly caught in a situation with no alternatives.

s: 금상첨화 means 'icing on the cake / making a good thing even better' in Korean
e: '금상첨화' literally means 'adding flowers to embroidery' — a four-character Chinese idiom describing something that makes a good situation even better.

s: 본인 means 'the person themselves / the individual in question' in Korean
e: '본인' means the person themselves — it emphasizes direct personal identity, used in formal or legal language to indicate the individual concerned.

s: 가하다 means 'to inflict / apply (force, harm)' in Korean
e: '가하다' means to apply or inflict something — pressure, force, harm, or punishment — onto someone or something.

s: 얼마간 means 'a certain amount / somewhat' in Korean
e: '얼마간' means some amount or a certain degree — it indicates an unspecified but real quantity or extent.

s: 눈뜨다 means 'to awaken / to open one's eyes (to something)' in Korean
e: '눈뜨다' means to open one's eyes or awaken — both literally waking up and figuratively becoming aware of something.

s: 따름 means 'nothing but / that is all' in Korean
e: '따름' means 'merely' or 'that is all' — it appears at the end of a sentence to emphasize there is nothing more to it.

s: 썩 means 'not very / not particularly (used with negatives)' in Korean
e: '썩' usually appears in negative sentences to mean 'not very' or 'not particularly.' In positive contexts it can mean exceedingly, but the negative use is far more common.

s: 반기 means 'half a year / a semester' in Korean
e: '반기' means half a year — it is used in financial and academic contexts, such as 상반기 (first half of the year) and 하반기 (second half of the year).

s: 돋치다 means 'to stick out / protrude' in Korean
e: '돋치다' means to protrude or stick out — something that rises above or beyond its surroundings.

s: 일선 means 'the front line / first line' in Korean
e: '일선' means the front line or first line of action — used for military front lines and for workers directly on the ground in their field.

s: 위주 means 'centered on / with priority given to' in Korean
e: '위주' means giving priority to something or centering activity around it — 학생 위주 means student-centered.

s: 보름 means 'fifteen days / the full moon period' in Korean
e: '보름' means fifteen days, and also refers to the full moon — 정월 대보름 is the Great Full Moon festival on the 15th day of the first lunar month.

s: 영하 means 'below zero / sub-zero temperature' in Korean
e: '영하' means below zero degrees on the temperature scale — 영하 10도 means minus 10 degrees Celsius.

s: 쭉 means 'all in one stretch / continuously' in Korean
e: '쭉' means doing something in one continuous stretch without stopping — reading straight through, walking all the way, etc.

s: 씩씩하다 means 'brave and spirited / bold and vigorous' in Korean
e: '씩씩하다' means brave, bold, and vigorous — describing someone who carries themselves with confidence and energy.

s: 어업 means 'fishing industry' in Korean
e: '어업' means the fishing industry — the business of catching fish and other aquatic life for commercial purposes.

s: 가변 means 'variable / changeable' in Korean
e: '가변' means variable or changeable — capable of being altered. Used in technical and formal contexts, such as 가변 금리 (variable interest rate).

s: 살피다 means 'to look over carefully / examine' in Korean
e: '살피다' means to carefully look over, examine, or keep an eye on something or someone.

s: 등지다 means 'to turn one's back on / fall out with' in Korean
e: '등지다' means to turn one's back on someone, to abandon or fall out with them — a breaking of a relationship or departure.

s: 단일 means 'single / unified (one kind only)' in Korean
e: '단일' means single or unified — consisting of one type only, without diversity. Used in contexts like 단일 민족 (single ethnicity) or 단일 시장 (single market).

s: 연합 means 'alliance / union' in Korean
e: '연합' means a union or alliance — multiple parties joining together as one. Used in contexts like 연합군 (allied forces) or 연합팀 (combined team).

s: 변덕 means 'capriciousness / fickleness' in Korean
e: '변덕' means fickleness or capriciousness — changing moods or decisions unpredictably. 변덕스럽다 means to be fickle or moody.

s: 별개 means 'separate / a different matter entirely' in Korean
e: '별개' means a separate or distinct matter — not related to what was just discussed. Often used as '별개의 문제' (a separate issue).

s: 수가 means 'medical fee / reimbursement point' in Korean
e: '수가' means a medical charge or point used to calculate healthcare reimbursement in Korea's national health insurance system.

s: 발열 means 'heat generation / fever' in Korean
e: '발열' means heat generation or the production of heat, and in medical usage refers to developing a fever.

s: 대개 means 'mostly / in most cases' in Korean
e: '대개' means mostly or in most cases — it describes the typical or general situation.

s: 오기 means 'fighting spirit / stubborn determination' in Korean
e: '오기' means a strong, stubborn determination that refuses to give up — an indomitable spirit fueled by pride and will.

s: 내다보다 means 'to look out (from inside)' in Korean
e: '내다보다' means to look outward from inside a space — looking out of a window or door.

s: 이만저만 means 'no small amount / not just a little (used in negatives)' in Korean
e: '이만저만' is used almost exclusively in negative constructions: '이만저만이 아니다' means 'it's no small thing' — it's more than one might think.

s: 아무개 means 'so-and-so / a certain unnamed person' in Korean
e: '아무개' is used as a placeholder name for an unnamed or unspecified person — equivalent to 'so-and-so' or 'Mr. X'.
